module_map.responsibility: HTTP request/response abstraction layer for Django's core I/O
WSGI/ASGI application entry points that coordinate middleware chain and view dispatch
Middleware components that process requests and responses; applied as a stack around view execution
URL routing and configuration; maps URL patterns to view callables
View layer and generic class-based views; application logic entry point after routing
Database abstraction and ORM for query execution, transactions, and model management
Core utilities and exception definitions; foundational services for all modules
Template rendering engine; converts template strings and context to output
Signal dispatcher for loose coupling between components; enables cross-module event publishing
Shared utility functions and helpers; modules include text processing, encoding, text extraction

pipeline_trace.description: WSGI/ASGI application receives raw HTTP request from web server (environ dict or ASGI scope/receive dict)
Handler converts raw WSGI/ASGI dict into HttpRequest object with parsed GET/POST/FILES/META
Middleware chain processes request before view; each middleware can modify request or short-circuit with response
URL resolver matches request.path against URLPatterns; extracts view function/class and URL parameters
View callable (function or class-based) executes business logic; may instantiate QuerySets and execute ORM queries
ORM converts QuerySet operations to SQL; executes via database backend connection
Template engine renders response content using context data from view and ORM results
Middleware processes response in reverse order; can modify headers, apply compression, caching
Response object serialized to WSGI response tuple or ASGI send dict; sent to web server and client

cross_module_hubs.reason: Central I/O abstraction; all request/response processing flows through HttpRequest and HttpResponse objects. Middleware, views, handlers, URLs, and template engine all depend on http module for request/response contract.
Application entry point and coordinator; orchestrates middleware chain, URL resolution, view dispatch, and exception handling. Houses BaseHandler that all WSGI/ASGI adapters inherit from.
ORM and database abstraction hub; accessed by views, models, migrations, and transaction context managers. Encapsulates backend-specific database interaction and query compilation.
Foundation module; exceptions, validators, signals, and utilities referenced across all other modules. Defines core exception hierarchy and validation contracts.
Routing hub; handlers depend on url resolution, and URL resolver instantiates view callables. Critical for mapping HTTP requests to application logic.

change_proposal.feature_summary: Add async-compatible structured logging middleware that captures per-request metadata (view name, ORM query count, response time) and emits it as structured log entries (JSON-formatted or standard library logging with context vars).
new_types: StructuredLoggingMiddleware (class): subclasses BaseMiddleware or implements __call__/__init__ contract
RequestLogContext (dataclass): holds per-request metadata (view_name, query_count, start_time, duration, response_status)
QueryCountTracker (context manager or signal-based tracker): integrates with django.db.connection.queries or django.test.utils.CaptureQueriesContext pattern
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow existing middleware patterns from django/middleware/common.py and django/middleware/csrf.py: (1) subclass object with __init__ and __call__ methods, (2) process_request/process_response hooks or direct __call__ implementation, (3) integrate with HttpRequest/HttpResponse contracts from django/http/, (4) use django.dispatch.Signal or context vars (PEP 567) for cross-module communication of ORM query count.
change_proposal.integration_point: Install as standard middleware in settings.MIDDLEWARE list after django.middleware.common.CommonMiddleware. Hook into request/response cycle via middleware protocol: __call__(self, request) -> response. For query counting, use django.db.connection.queries (requires DEBUG=True) or signal-based approach via django.db.models.signals (pre_save, post_save) to track ORM operations. Store request-local context using threading.local() (WSGI) or contextvars (ASGI async support).
risks: Query counting accuracy: django.db.connection.queries is not populated in async contexts and is debug-only; may require additional instrumentation (e.g., signal-based tracking or query wrapper patches).
Performance overhead: structured logging with context capture on every request may add measurable latency; mitigation: make verbosity configurable and logging level conditional.
Async compatibility: contextvars-based approach required for async views to avoid threading.local() conflicts; existing middleware may not use contextvars, requiring careful coordination.

Signal/context var interference: using django.dispatch.Signal or contextvars for cross-module communication adds implicit coupling; changes to signal signatures or context var names could break downstream logging handlers.

Maintainability: query counting logic may need updates if Django's ORM query execution path changes (db.backends refactoring, async ORM additions); consider using public instrumentation APIs rather than private _queries attribute.

Third-party middleware interaction: middleware execution order matters; if logging middleware runs before/after CSRF or common middleware, timing metrics may include/exclude those costs unpredictably.

tool_usage.params: root path /tmp/benchmark-repos/django with max_depth=2, summary=true to identify top-level module structure (apps, conf, contrib, core, db, dispatch, forms, http, middleware, template, urls, utils, views)
django/ core module structure to identify subdirectories (core/handlers, core/cache, core/management, etc.) and module relationships
django/http to extract request/response classes and multipart parsing logic (5 files, 2450 LOC)
django/middleware to identify middleware stack components and their responsibilities (10 files, 1231 LOC)
django/urls to understand routing layer modules (resolvers, conf, converters) and URLPattern/URLResolver abstractions
django/db to map ORM layer structure (transaction, utils, models, backends subdirectories)
django/views to extract view layer modules (generic views, debug views, decorators)
django/core to identify core utilities and exception hierarchy (8 files, 1704 LOC)
django/core/handlers to identify WSGI/ASGI entry points and BaseHandler coordination logic (5 files, 1133 LOC)
django/middleware (summary=true) - extracted directory metadata; file-level analysis limited by parser constraints
